title: Rioux Test for Catecholates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3la1e","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8v58v","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"62p1h","text":"Modified from Rioux, C., Jordan, D., \u0026 Rattray, J. B. (1983). Colorimetric determination of catechol siderophores in microbial cultures. Analytical Biochemistry,133(1), 163-169. doi:10.1016/0003-2697(83)90238-5","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":137,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 0.2 mL 20% sulfuric acid to 2.3 mL H 2 O and mix
Add 1 mL sample (or H 2 O for blank, DHBA for standard curve) undetermined
Add 0.1 mL 1% ferric ammonium citrate (in 0.09 N H 2 SO 4 ) and mix undetermined
Add 0.4 mL 2 M ammonium fluoride and mix undetermined
Add 0.4 mL 1% 1,10-penanthroline and mix undetermined Add 0.4 mL water for turbidity blank.
Add 0.6 mL 3 M hexamethyl tetramine undetermined
Cool to room temperature and read in spectrophotometer at 510 nm
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
